step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture is initiated by a scheduled CloudWatch Event that triggers AWS Step Functions. Step Functions then orchestrates a workflow consisting of three Lambda functions. The first Lambda function queries the third-party drone imaging service via API to check for new images. The second Lambda function copies these processed images (orthomosaics, terrain, and elevation models) from the third-party system into an Amazon S3 bucket. A third Lambda function creates raster proxy files pointing to these S3 assets and writes them to the on-premise Database. Finally, the on-premise mapping Server accesses these images from S3 seamlessly by using the raster proxies via AWS Storage Gateway.

graph.name: Marathon Oil Serverless Drone Imagery Ingestion and Mapping Architecture
graph.notes: Serverless drone image ingestion pipeline that automatically loads orthomosaics and digital terrain models from a third-party drone capture service into Amazon S3, using raster proxies in an on-premise database and AWS Storage Gateway to allow legacy on-premise mapping software to read S3 objects directly.

nodes.name: 3rd Party
nodes.notes: Represents the external drone imagery capturing software and pilots.

nodes.name: Cloudwatch Event
nodes.notes: The scheduled trigger event modeled as a CloudWatch Event.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: Stores the processed imagery and index files.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Executes the integration logic to fetch images, save to S3, and create raster proxies.

nodes.name: Storage Gateway
nodes.notes: Allows the on-premise server to access images in S3 directly.

nodes.name: Database
nodes.notes: On-premise database where raster proxies are stored.

edges.notes: The scheduled CloudWatch Event triggers the Step Functions workflow.
Step Functions orchestrates and invokes the Lambda functions.
The first Lambda function calls the 3rd Party API to check for new images.
Lambda function writes the fetched images into the S3 bucket.
Visual representation of the image files being transferred and stored in the S3 bucket.
The third Lambda function writes the raster proxy URL into the on-premise database.
The on-premise mapping server accesses files through AWS Storage Gateway.
Storage Gateway connects the on-premise mapping software to the S3 bucket.
